back_translation: This is the list of the Israelites—the heads of families, the commanders of thousands and hundreds, and their officers who served the king in every matter concerning the divisions that rotated on military duty each month throughout the year. Each division had 24,000 men:
Jashobeam son of Zabdiel was in charge of the first division, for the first month. His division had 24,000 men.
He was a descendant of Perez and chief of all the army commanders for the first month.
In charge of the division for the second month was Dodai the Ahohite, with Mikloth as his deputy. His division had 24,000 men.
The third army commander, for the third month, was Benaiah son of Jehoiada the priest. His division had 24,000 men.
This Benaiah was a mighty man among the Thirty and was over the Thirty, and his son Ammizabad was in charge of his division.
The fourth, for the fourth month, was Asahel the brother of Joab, and his son Zebadiah was commander after him. His division had 24,000 men.
The fifth, for the fifth month, was the commander Shamhuth the Izrahite. His division had 24,000 men.
The sixth, for the sixth month, was Ira son of Ikkesh the Tekoite. His division had 24,000 men.
The seventh, for the seventh month, was Helez the Pelonite, an Ephraimite. His division had 24,000 men.
The eighth, for the eighth month, was Sibbecai the Hushathite, of the family of Zerah. His division had 24,000 men.
The ninth, for the ninth month, was Abiezer the Anathothite, a Benjamite. His division had 24,000 men.
The tenth, for the tenth month, was Maharai the Netophathite, of the family of Zerah. His division had 24,000 men.
The eleventh, for the eleventh month, was Benaiah the Pirathonite, an Ephraimite. His division had 24,000 men.
The twelfth, for the twelfth month, was Heldai the Netophathite, of the family of Othniel. His division had 24,000 men.
These were the officers in charge of the tribes of Israel: over the Reubenites, Eliezer son of Zichri; over the Simeonites, Shephatiah son of Maacah;
over Levi, Hashabiah son of Kemuel; over Aaron's descendants, Zadok;
over Judah, Elihu, one of David's brothers; over Issachar, Omri son of Michael;
over Zebulun, Ishmaiah son of Obadiah; over Naphtali, Jerimoth son of Azriel;
over the Ephraimites, Hoshea son of Azaziah; over the half-tribe of Manasseh, Joel son of Pedaiah;
over the other half-tribe of Manasseh in Gilead, Iddo son of Zechariah; over Benjamin, Jaasiel son of Abner;
and over Dan, Azarel son of Jeroham. These were the leaders of the tribes of Israel.
David did not count those twenty years old or younger, because the LORD had said He would make Israel as numerous as the stars of the sky.
Joab son of Zeruiah began to count but did not finish. Because of this census, wrath came upon Israel, and the number was not recorded in the Book of the Chronicles of King David.
Azmaveth son of Adiel was in charge of the royal storehouses. Jonathan son of Uzziah was in charge of the storehouses in the country—in the cities, villages, and fortresses.
Ezri son of Chelub was in charge of the workers who tilled the fields.
Shimei the Ramathite was in charge of the vineyards. Zabdi the Shiphmite was in charge of the produce of the vineyards for the wine vats.
Baal-hanan the Gederite was in charge of the olive and sycamore trees in the foothills. Joash was in charge of the stores of olive oil.
Shitrai the Sharonite was in charge of the herds grazing in Sharon. Shaphat son of Adlai was in charge of the herds in the valleys.
Obil the Ishmaelite was in charge of the camels. Jehdeiah the Meronothite was in charge of the donkeys.
Jaziz the Hagrite was in charge of the flocks. All these were officials in charge of King David's property.
Jonathan, David's uncle, was a counselor, a man of insight and a scribe. Jehiel son of Hachmoni attended to the king's sons.
Ahithophel was the king's counselor. Hushai the Archite was the king's friend.
Ahithophel was succeeded by Jehoiada son of Benaiah, and by Abiathar. Joab was the commander of the king's army.
